Start cooking rice in a rice cooker or pot. Take a thawed salmon filet, remove skin, and sprinkle salt on all sides. Add oil to a hot pan and place salmon to develop a sear. Add a drizzle of oil to the uneven side and flip. Press with spatula if needed. Sear for 7 minutes or until cooked through. Set aside to rest.

Step 2: Pickle the vegetables
Add julienned carrots and thinly sliced serrano peppers to a glass jar. Add salt and a pinch of MSG. Pour over water and vinegar mixture, ensuring vegetables are submerged. These will pickle over time and store well in the fridge.

Step 3: Assemble the box
Place reheatable items in the bottom layer. Add salmon, followed by 100 g cooked rice in the center. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.\n\nStep 2: Dredge the chicken\nPrepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.\n\nStep 3: Fry the chicken\nAt the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.\n\nStep 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich\nCombine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. "pcsv": ["carb"]}, {"name": "cabbage", "amount": "thinly sliced", "pcsv": ["veggie"]}], "instructions": "Step 1: Flatten the chicken & make the sauce
Set a wok or deep vessel over medium-low heat and add enough oil to fry the chicken, about 2 inches deep. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.

Step 2: Dredge the chicken
Prepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.

Step 3: Fry the chicken
At the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.

Step 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich
Combine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. To assemble the sandwich, spread the mayo sauce on the bread, followed by the katsu-ish sauce and thinly sliced green cabbage. Top with the chicken and add more sauce over the top. Best matches from the recipe book:
- Chicken and Bok Choy Stir-Fry / 鸡肉炒青菜 — Cookbook recipe. You already have chicken and rice; it needs bok choy, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, oyster sauce. Good base if you want a savory rice dinner, though it’s not BBQ-flavored.
- Restaurant-Style Chicken Fried Rice / 饭店炒饭 — Cookbook recipe. You already have chicken and rice; it needs egg, soy sauce, sesame oil, peas, carrots, green onions, garlic. Easy crowd-pleaser for 8, but again not BBQ-specific.

If you want, I can next turn this into a true BBQ dinner for 8 by suggesting the missing BBQ sauce, vegetables, and a shopping list grounded in store products."}}, {"event_type": "done", "data": results.error: The three recipes collectively require many non-staple, non-user-provided ingredients. The Za'atar Bowl needs specialty spices (za'atar, sumac, garam masala), pomegranate seeds, feta, and almonds. The Bento Box ignores broccoli entirely and needs hummus, havarti, olives, pita, and peppers. The Lo Mein is the best match but still requires shaoxing wine, sesame oil, oyster sauce, and multiple extra vegetables (squash, bell peppers, bean sprouts). While garlic, onion, and soy sauce are borderline staples, the overall ingredient load across all recipes far exceeds what the user has plus reasonable pantry staples.

assertion.value:
The user provided these ingredients: {{input}}. Common pantry staples (salt, pepper, oil, garlic, onion, basic spices, soy sauce, vinegar) are acceptable.
Score 1-5:
5 = All ingredients in recipes are either user-provided, pantry staples, or minor acceptable extras (1-2 per recipe).
3 = Several unlisted non-staple ingredients appear but don't dominate.
1 = Recipes are loaded with ingredients the user didn't mention (e.g., needing butter, cream, specialty produce, fresh herbs not named).
Return the score.

componentResults.reason: The three recipes collectively require many non-staple, non-user-provided ingredients. The Za'atar Bowl needs specialty spices (za'atar, sumac, garam masala), pomegranate seeds, feta, and almonds. The Bento Box ignores broccoli entirely and needs hummus, havarti, olives, pita, and peppers. The Lo Mein is the best match but still requires shaoxing wine, sesame oil, oyster sauce, and multiple extra vegetables (squash, bell peppers, bean sprouts). While garlic, onion, and soy sauce are borderline staples, the overall ingredient load across all recipes far exceeds what the user has plus reasonable pantry staples.

the sauce\nSet a wok or deep vessel over medium-low heat and add enough oil to fry the chicken, about 2 inches deep. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.\n\nStep 2: Dredge the chicken\nPrepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.\n\nStep 3: Fry the chicken\nAt the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.\n\nStep 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich\nCombine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. To assemble the sandwich, spread the mayo sauce on the bread, followed by the katsu-ish sauce and thinly sliced green cabbage. Top with the chicken and add more sauce over the top. Set a wok or deep vessel over medium-low heat and add enough oil to fry the chicken, about 2 inches deep. Flatten the chicken breast with a rolling pin, cast iron pan, or another blunt object. Optionally, cut the chicken into a square to fit the bread. Season both sides generously with salt. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix together. Set aside for serving.

Step 2: Dredge the chicken
Prepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.

Step 3: Fry the chicken
At the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. When the internal temperature reaches 155°F/68°C, transfer the chicken to a paper towel on a wire rack, dab the excess oil, and then move it to the wire rack so it doesn't get soggy. While the chicken fries, prepare the sandwich toppings, wash the cutting board and breading plates, and wipe down your work surfaces. Always clean as you go.

Step 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich
Combine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. To assemble the sandwich, spread the mayo sauce on the bread, followed by the katsu-ish sauce and thinly sliced green cabbage. Top with the chicken and add more sauce over the top. Step 2: Dredge the chicken
Prepare the breading station with 3 plates or trays: one with flour and cornstarch, one with egg, and one with panko bread crumbs. Lightly coat both sides of the chicken in the flour, then move it to the egg, and finally, coat it with the bread crumbs. Press the bread crumbs into the chicken to ensure even coverage and sticking.

Step 3: Fry the chicken
At the stove, use a thermometer to verify the oil is between 325-350°F/162-176°C. Slowly drop the chicken into the oil until it becomes golden brown. Fry the chicken for about 7 to 8 minutes, slightly agitating it throughout. Step 4: Make the mayo & assemble the sandwich
Combine the mayo, lemon juice, garlic, MSG and salt in a bowl and mix it together. Add spinach leaves and salt, sauté until barely wilted. Transfer entire contents to blender (or use immersion blender in pot). Add just enough water to blend until smooth, then pour back into pot. 3. Thinly slice the steak into strips across the grain. Add all marinated beef ingredients to a bowl and mix until well coated. Set aside to marinate while you prepare the sauce or other ingredients.\n2. Add all black pepper sauce ingredients to a bowl and mix together until well combined. This sauce makes enough for 2 stir-fries and can be made ahead and stored in the fridge.\n3. Set a wok or large pan over high heat and add a drizzle of neutral oil. Quickly saute the garlic and shallots for 45 seconds or until fragrant. Add the onions and peppers and stir fry until slightly softened and browned. Remove the vegetable mixture from the pan and set aside in a bowl.\n4. Add another drizzle of oil to the wok. Once hot, add in the marinated beef slices and let those sizzle and brown on one side without moving. After 2 minutes, start moving it around, trying to get browning on all sides. Once the beef is browned and almost cooked through (but not overcooked), add the vegetables back in.\n5. Add about half of the black pepper sauce and stir it all together to spread the flavors around. If you want the dish to be saucier, you can add more at this point. Lastly, dial in the consistency of the sauce. Add a splash of cornstarch slurry and bring the sauce to a simmer. Check the thickness and repeat until you've reached your perfect consistency. 3. Set a wok or large pan over high heat and add a drizzle of neutral oil. Quickly saute the garlic and shallots for 45 seconds or until fragrant. Add the onions and peppers and stir fry until slightly softened and browned. Remove the vegetable mixture from the pan and set aside in a bowl.
4. Add another drizzle of oil to the wok. Once hot, add in the marinated beef slices and let those sizzle and brown on one side without moving. After 2 minutes, start moving it around, trying to get browning on all sides. Once the beef is browned and almost cooked through (but not overcooked), add the vegetables back in.
5. Add about half of the black pepper sauce and stir it all together to spread the flavors around. If you want the dish to be saucier, you can add more at this point.
